info.title: MS365 Access API
info.description: API for accessing Microsoft 365 services (Email, Calendar, OneDrive)

parameters.name: user

schema.description: UPN of a shared mailbox you have Full Access to.
schema.title: User

parameters.description: UPN of a shared mailbox you have Full Access to.

get.description:
Resolve a well-known folder name to its folder object including ID.

Well-known folder names include: inbox, drafts, sentitems, deleteditems,
junkemail, archive, outbox, etc.

This is useful because MS Graph API can cache results when querying by
well-known name. Using the actual folder ID ensures fresh results.

parameters.name: user

schema.description: UPN of a shared mailbox you have Full Access to.
schema.title: User

parameters.description: UPN of a shared mailbox you have Full Access to.

get.description:
List messages from a folder.

Note: MS Graph API can cache results when querying folders by well-known name.
This endpoint resolves folder names to IDs internally to ensure fresh results.

parameters.name: folder

schema.description: Well-known folder name (inbox, archive, junkemail, etc.). Will be resolved to folder_id internally to avoid MS Graph caching issues.
schema.title: Folder

parameters.description: Well-known folder name (inbox, archive, junkemail, etc.). Will be resolved to folder_id internally to avoid MS Graph caching issues.

parameters.name: folder_id

schema.description: Actual folder ID. Takes precedence over 'folder' if both provided.
schema.title: Folder Id

parameters.description: Actual folder ID. Takes precedence over 'folder' if both provided.

post.description:
Create a new draft message in Drafts folder (does not send).

Requires write:draft permission. To send the draft later, use
POST /mail/messages/{draft_id}/send (requires write:mail).

post.description:
Move a message to a destination folder.

The destination_folder_id can be either an actual folder ID or a well-known
folder name (inbox, archive, junkemail, etc.).

If verify=True (default), the response will include a 'verified' field
indicating whether the message's parentFolderId matches the destination.

parameters.name: verify

schema.description: Verify the move by checking parentFolderId after

get.description:
List messages grouped by conversationId, ordered by most recent thread activity.

Returns threads with: conversationId, subject, messages[], latestDateTime, messageCount.

post.description:
Add a file attachment to a message or draft.

Accepts base64-encoded file content. Maximum size ~3MB
(MS Graph limit for single-request attachments).

Requires write:draft permission.

parameters.name: user

schema.description: UPN of a shared mailbox you have Full Access to. Default: your own mailbox.
schema.title: User

put.description:
Replace the content of an existing file (keeps the same item ID).

OneDrive/SharePoint preserves version history, so the previous content
is still accessible via the file's version history.

parameters.name: user

schema.description: UPN of another user whose OneDrive you have access to. Default: your own OneDrive.
schema.title: User

parameters.description: UPN of another user whose OneDrive you have access to. Default: your own OneDrive.

get.description:
Resolve a SharePoint site by hostname/path.

Example: /sharepoint/sites/contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Finance
List document libraries (drives) for a SharePoint site.
List children of a folder in a SharePoint drive.
Download file content from SharePoint. Optionally convert format (e.g. format=pdf).

put.description:
Replace the content of an existing file in SharePoint (keeps the same item ID).

SharePoint preserves version history, so the previous content is still
accessible via the file's version history.

Upload a file to a SharePoint site's default drive.

Uses /sites/{site_id}/drive path to avoid b!-prefixed drive IDs.

get.description: Get item metadata from a SharePoint drive.

patch.description: Rename a file or folder in a SharePoint drive.

get.description: List version history for a file in a SharePoint drive.
Download a specific historical version of a SharePoint file.

patch.description: Move a file or folder to a different folder in a SharePoint drive.

get.description: Search within a SharePoint site's default drive.
Resolve a SharePoint sharing URL to item metadata + site_id + item_id.
Report whether the file is checked out (co-authoring is not a checkout).

post.description: Create a workbook session. Use the returned id for subsequent calls.

get.description: List worksheets in the workbook.
List tables in the workbook.
Read a cell range, e.g. sheet='Sheet1' address='A1:C10'.

patch.description:
Write a 2D array of values into a range.

Body: {"sheet": "Sheet1", "address": "A1:B2", "values": [["x", 1], ["y", 2]]}

With no session_id and auto_session=True (default), the server opens and
closes a persistent session around the write for a clean one-call edit.

post.description:
Append rows to a table. Body: {"values": [["east", "pear", 4]]}.

Auto-sessions around the write when no session_id is given (see update_range).

get.description: List all Power BI workspaces the user has access to.
List datasets in a workspace.
List tables in a dataset. Falls back to DAX INFO.TABLES() for standard datasets.

post.description:
Execute a DAX query against a dataset.

Requires Power BI Premium or Premium Per User capacity.
Returns columns and rows from the first result table.

get.description: List reports in a workspace.

post.description:
Trigger a dataset refresh. Returns immediately — poll GET .../refreshes for status.

Power BI Pro: 8 refreshes/day max. Premium: 48/day.

get.description:
List recent dataset refresh history (status, start/end time, type).

Use after triggering a refresh to check if it completed or failed.

parameters.name: top

schema.description: Number of recent refreshes to return
schema.title: Top

parameters.description: Number of recent refreshes to return

get.description: List all API keys (admin only). Raw key values are never returned.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Api Keys Get

post.description:
Create a new API key. The raw key is returned only once.

Supply either `tier` (e.g. "openclaw") to use a predefined permission set,
or supply an explicit `permissions` list. Providing both is an error.

patch.description: Update an API key's name, permissions, or active status.

delete.description: Revoke (deactivate) an API key. Does not delete it from the DB.

ApiKeyCreated.title: ApiKeyCreated
ApiKeyCreated.description: Response when a new key is created — includes the raw key (shown only once).
